_note: A generic 'research → write → publish' starter set. Import it in one call to get a coherent operation graph you can build on. See examples/README.md.

operations.name: idea.capture
operations.summary: Turn a rough thought into a scoped, answerable question
operations.prompt: Take the user's rough idea and sharpen it into one specific, answerable question plus the audience it serves and why it matters now. Return {question, audience, why}.

operations.name: source.gather
operations.summary: Collect credible sources for a question
operations.prompt: For the given question, find several credible, independent sources. For each return {title, url, oneLineClaim}. Prefer primary sources; never fabricate a URL.

operations.name: fact.check
operations.summary: Verify claims against the gathered sources
operations.prompt: Cross-check each claim you intend to use against at least two of the gathered sources. Flag anything unsupported. Return {claim, supported: true|false, sources}. Pull source.gather first.

operations.name: draft.write
operations.summary: Write a first draft from verified material
operations.prompt: Write a clear first draft that answers idea.capture's question using only fact.check-verified claims, in the tone from knowledge voice.tone. Cite sources inline. Return the draft.

operations.name: publish.ready
operations.summary: Full pipeline: capture, research, verify, draft, checklist
operations.prompt: Run the whole pipeline: idea.capture, source.gather, fact.check, draft.write. Then produce a short publish checklist (title, one-line summary, sources listed, open questions). Return {draft, checklist}.

knowledge.value: Clear, specific, and honest. Short sentences. Lead with the point, support it with a cited fact, and never overclaim.
Deliver long outputs as a clean, organized page: a clear title, sensible sections, and sources listed at the end.
